description: Example MCP configuration for Windsurf Cascade - Docker-based MCP servers with HTTP transport

metadata.description: Source of truth for code, PRs, and CI state

metadata.note: Requires docker compose up -d mcp-sonarqube. Token must be in Authorization header for HTTP mode.
[PLACEHOLDER] Verify MCP server implementation exists. Not yet configured in docker-compose.yml.
Requires docker compose --profile optional up -d mcp-jira

metadata.description: Task and issue tracking (alternative to JIRA)
metadata.note: Requires docker compose --profile optional up -d mcp-clickup
[NOT CONFIGURED] No Docker service configured. Add to docker-compose.yml if needed.
[NOT CONFIGURED] No Docker service configured. Add to docker-compose.yml if needed.
[NOT CONFIGURED] No Docker service configured. Add to docker-compose.yml if needed.

remote_http_mcp_format.serverUrl: Required. The URL endpoint for the remote MCP server (must end with /mcp)
remote_http_mcp_format.headers: Optional. Custom headers to send with requests (e.g., Authorization)
remote_http_mcp_format.disabled: Optional. Set to true to disable the server
remote_http_mcp_format.note: Do NOT use 'url' or 'transport' properties - use 'serverUrl' only per Windsurf documentation

important: [DOCKER-BASED] This configuration uses serverUrl to connect to Docker-based MCP servers.
[PREREQUISITES] Run 'docker compose up -d' to start MCP servers before using Windsurf.
[CREDENTIALS] Set credentials in .env file (see .env.example), not in this config.
[SONARQUBE AUTH] SonarQube requires Authorization header with token. Use ${env:SONARQUBE_TOKEN} for interpolation.
[CONFIG INTERPOLATION] Use ${env:VAR_NAME} to reference environment variables in headers.
[PROJECT-SPECIFIC] Enable only servers for systems you actually use.
[TOOL BUDGET] Windsurf has a limit on total enabled tools. Start with 2-3 essential servers.
setup_steps: 1. Copy .env.example to .env and fill in credentials
2. Set environment variable: export SONARQUBE_TOKEN=your_token_here (in ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc)
3. Start MCP servers: docker compose up -d mcp-github mcp-slack mcp-sonarqube
4. Verify servers are running: docker compose ps
6. Remove 'disabled: true' from servers you want to use
7. Restart Windsurf to load the configuration
8. Test each server before enabling the next
9. Update AGENTS.md with MCP source-of-truth mappings

essential: mcp-github (port 8102) - Start with: docker compose up -d mcp-github
mcp-slack (port 8103) - Start with: docker compose up -d mcp-slack
recommended: mcp-sonarqube (port 8104) - Start with: docker compose up -d mcp-sonarqube
optional: mcp-jira (port 8100) - Start with: docker compose --profile optional up -d mcp-jira
mcp-clickup (port 8101) - Start with: docker compose --profile optional up -d mcp-clickup
not_configured: datadog, sentry, confluence - Not configured in docker-compose.yml

docker_containers.github: GITHUB_TOKEN - Set in .env for Docker container
docker_containers.slack: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_TEAM_ID - Set in .env for Docker container
docker_containers.sonarqube: SONARQUBE_TOKEN, SONARQUBE_ORG/SONARQUBE_URL - Set in .env for Docker container
docker_containers.jira: JIRA_URL, JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_TOKEN - Set in .env for Docker container
docker_containers.clickup: CLICKUP_API_TOKEN, CLICKUP_TEAM_ID - Set in .env for Docker container

windsurf_config.sonarqube: SONARQUBE_TOKEN - Set as shell environment variable for ${env:SONARQUBE_TOKEN} interpolation
windsurf_config.note: Use ${env:VAR_NAME} syntax in headers to reference shell environment variables

documentation.docker_setup: See docs/mcp-docker-setup.md for complete Docker setup guide
documentation.mcp_integration: See docs/mcp-integration.md for MCP architecture and design

npx_based.note: For npx-based MCP servers (stdio transport), use command/args/env properties

docker_stdio.note: For Docker with stdio transport, use command: docker with run -i --rm

remote_http.note: For remote HTTP MCPs (this config), use serverUrl and optional headers

comparison.docker_http: Isolated, persistent, team-friendly, requires Docker Compose setup
comparison.docker_stdio: Isolated, ephemeral, requires Docker but simpler config
comparison.npx_stdio: Quick setup, auto-updates, requires Node.js, good for local testing
